ribbondesc: Advancing the frontiers of computational multiphysics through innovative research and cutting-edge numerical methods.

High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.title: High-Performance Computing & Solver Development
High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.subtitle: Adaptive, Scalable, and Multiphysics-Ready
High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.overview: Core lab effort: developing exascale-ready solvers built on Adaptive Mesh Refinement (AMR) and a Hybrid Sharp–Diffuse Interface Framework. These solver technologies underpin our shock hydrocodes, all-speed CFD, and multiscale couplers.
currentProjects: AMR-enabled hydrocodes and shock solvers
Density-based all-speed solver framework with low-Mach preconditioning
Hybrid Sharp–Diffuse Interface methods integrated with AMR
impact: Exascale-ready implementations for large HPC centers
Unified solver frameworks that span Mach regimes and multiphase problems
Reusable libraries and software components for group projects

High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.title: In-House Multi-Material Eulerian Hydrocode
High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.subtitle: High-Strain-Rate Physics and Shock Dynamics
High-Performance Computing & Solver Deve.overview: Scalable multi-material Eulerian hydrocode for shock propagation, impact and fragmentation problems. Designed to resolve sharp features while remaining robust in mixed-material zones using the hybrid interface approach.
currentProjects: Modeling shock initiation in energetic materials
Impact response of layered protective structures
High-order methods and AMR for shock-focused multiphysics
impact: Accurate prediction of shock–material interactions
Improved designs for protective systems and planetary entry models
Bridging experiments and simulations for validation

Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.title: Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems
Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.subtitle: Initiation, Hot-Spot Physics, and Multiscale Reactivity
Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.overview: Multiscale, multiphysics study of energetic materials from atomistic chemistry to continuum-scale initiation and run-to-detonation behavior. We combine DFT, MD, KMC and continuum reactive flow models for predictive capabilities.
currentProjects: Hot-spot formation and growth in heterogeneous energetic materials
Reactive burn models coupled to hydrocodes
Multiscale parameter passing: DFT → MD → continuum
impact: Mechanistic insight into initiation and sensitivity
Improved constitutive/reaction models for engineering simulations
Data-driven parameter calibration linking experiments and simulation

Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.title: Reactive Multiscale Couplers
Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.subtitle: DFT-to-Continuum Workflows
Energetic Materials & Reactive Systems.overview: Workflows that extract reaction pathways and rates from atomistic simulations and feed them into meso/continuum models for predictive reactive simulations under extreme loading.
currentProjects: Automated extraction of kinetics from DFT/MD
Kinetic Monte Carlo for mesoscale reaction networks
Uncertainty quantification for reactive model parameters
impact: Robust end-to-end reactive modeling chain
Reduced reliance on ad-hoc empirical parameters
Better predictive capability for energetic system design

Impact-Resistant & Protective Materials.title: Impact-Resistant & Protective Materials
Impact-Resistant & Protective Materials.subtitle: Multiscale Mechanics and Materials Design for Protection
Impact-Resistant & Protective Materials.overview: Design and simulation of materials and structures optimized for impact resistance — from microstructural tailoring to structural-scale response — using coupled multiscale mechanics and dynamic testing calibration.
currentProjects: Multiscale models for fiber-matrix composites under impact
Topology and microstructure optimization for energy absorption
Coupling experiments and simulations for validation
impact: Guides design of lighter, more effective protective systems
Translates microstructural design rules to engineering-scale performance
Supports defense and aerospace material qualification

Biophysical & Biomedical Flow Modeling.title: Biophysical & Biomedical Flow Modeling
Biophysical & Biomedical Flow Modeling.subtitle: Cardiovascular, Microfluidic, and Cellular-Scale Flows
Biophysical & Biomedical Flow Modeling.overview: Applying our all-speed solver technology and multiscale coupling to biofluids: cardiovascular flows, microfluidics, and transport in porous biological media. Emphasis on physiological fidelity and clinically relevant predictions.
currentProjects: Patient-specific cardiovascular flow simulations (low-Mach regime)
Microfluidic device modelling with interface-resolving methods
Transport and mixing of particulates/cells in complex geometries
impact: Supports translational bioengineering and device design
Bridges physiological measurements and predictive modelling
Enables quantitative hypotheses for experimental collaborators

Hypersonic & Reentry Physics.title: Hypersonic & Reentry Physics
Hypersonic & Reentry Physics.subtitle: Ablation, Aerothermochemistry, and Plasma–Material Interaction
Hypersonic & Reentry Physics.overview: Coupled study of high-enthalpy aerothermodynamics, surface ablation and chemistry, and plasma–material interactions encountered during hypersonic flight and planetary reentry.
currentProjects: Ablation and surface chemistry modelling for TPS materials
Aerothermochemical coupling in boundary layers at high enthalpy
Multiscale methods for gas-surface interaction kinetics
impact: Improves predictive models for thermal protection systems
Informs material selection and design for extreme environments
Enables joint experiments and simulation campaigns with hypersonics groups
